title: ranunculus nelsonii var. tenellus
intro: Ranunculus nelsonii var. tenellus, also known as Nelson's Ranunculus, is a rare and delicate flowering plant native to North America. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating rheumatism and as a skin wash for eczema and acne due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Ranunculus nelsonii var. tenellus can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, although it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes.
